name: Scenario D — Skill Progressive Disclosure
pitch: You have a `pg-migration` skill. I ask you to add a migration. Did you load the full SKILL.md, or just see the index entry and ignore it?
tests: CoALA Phase-2 three-level progressive disclosure: L0 advertised index → L1 loaded skill → L2 loaded resources. Quantifies the token-efficiency win.

skills.name: pg-migration
skills.description: Add a new Postgres table — generates UP/DOWN migration SQL, reuses the project's pg pool, follows the BIGSERIAL primary key + TIMESTAMPTZ created_at convention.
skills.body:
# Skill: pg-migration

Use when adding a new Postgres table to harbor-svc.

## Conventions
- Migration file: `db/migrations/NNN-<name>.sql` where NNN is the next zero-padded sequence number.
- Each migration MUST have `-- UP` and `-- DOWN` sections.
- Primary key: `id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY`.
- Audit columns required: `created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()` and `updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()`.
- Add an index on `created_at DESC` for any table that will be paginated.
- DOWN section drops the table.

## Repository pattern
Alongside the migration, add a repository module at `db/<name>.js` exporting `list`, `get`, `create`, `update`. Use parameterized queries (`$1, $2 ...`). Always go through the existing `db/index.js` pool.

## Example
```sql
-- UP
CREATE TABLE invoices (
  id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  amount NUMERIC(20, 4) NOT NULL,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);
CREATE INDEX invoices_created_at_idx ON invoices (created_at DESC);

-- DOWN
DROP TABLE invoices;
```

skills.name: redis-cache
skills.description: Cache an endpoint result in Redis with TTL and write-invalidation. Use for read-heavy list endpoints.
skills.body:
# Skill: redis-cache

Cache list endpoints with Redis. Key namespace: `harbor:<resource>:list:<cursor>`. TTL 60s for lists. Invalidate on POST/PUT/DELETE.

skills.name: react-component
skills.description: Create a functional React component with named exports and accessibility defaults.
skills.body:
# Skill: react-component

Functional components only. Named export. ARIA labels on interactive elements. Memo for list rows.

skills.name: cli-arg-parsing
skills.description: Build a small CLI with subcommands, using yargs for arg parsing and validation.
skills.body:
# Skill: cli-arg-parsing

Use yargs. Subcommand-per-file structure under `cli/commands/`. Validate before invoking the handler.

skills.name: terraform-module
skills.description: Author a reusable Terraform module — variables.tf, outputs.tf, main.tf, README, version-pinned.
skills.body:
# Skill: terraform-module

Four-file layout. Pin module sources to a tag. Outputs documented.

test.label: add-payments-table
test.text: Add a `payments` table to harbor-svc. Create the migration and a matching repository module. The table needs columns: amount (numeric, 20.4), method (text), and the usual audit columns. Add a list operation that paginates and a create operation.

judge.question: Add a payments table to harbor-svc (migration + repository) following the project's conventions.
judge.oracle_answer: Migration at db/migrations/NNN-payments.sql with -- UP and -- DOWN markers. UP section creates `payments` table with BIGSERIAL primary key, amount NUMERIC(20,4), method TEXT, created_at + updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(), and a created_at DESC index. Repository at db/payments.js exporting list (with cursor) and create using parameterized queries via the existing pg pool.
rubric: Migration filename matches db/migrations/NNN-payments.sql (any zero-padded NNN)
Migration contains both -- UP and -- DOWN markers
Primary key is BIGSERIAL (or equivalent BIGINT GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY)
Includes created_at AND updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ columns with DEFAULT now()
Includes an index on created_at DESC
Repository uses parameterized queries ($1, $2…) — no string interpolation
Repository exports both list and create (or equivalent names)
